overview.description: Global cybersecurity company using AI/ML for proactive threat detection and prevention
overview.description_long: ESET is a global leader in cybersecurity, combining AI-native technology with human expertise to protect businesses, critical infrastructure, and individuals from evolving threats. Founded in Slovakia in 1992, ESET operates a Prague office serving Czech Republic clients, an R&D lab in Brno, and 11 global R&D centers. Its PROTECT Platform integrates behavioral analysis, machine learning (since 1997), and the 2024-launched ESET AI Advisor for XDR-based incident response. Products span consumer antivirus (NOD32), endpoint protection, and enterprise threat intelligence.

history.origin_story: Founded in Bratislava in 1992 by Miroslav Trnka and Peter Paško, who in 1987 wrote NOD — one of the first PC antivirus programs — while Czechoslovakia was still under communist rule. After the fall of communism, they formalised ESET as a private company. Czech Republic subsidiary registered 2001, R&D lab in Brno and sales office in Prague (Praha 7).

key_milestones.event: NOD antivirus created by Trnka and Paško in Czechoslovakia
ESET formally founded as private company in Bratislava, Slovakia
Introduced heuristic analysis into detection engine
First use of neural networks / machine learning in products
ESET software spol. s r.o. established in Czech Republic (Prague)
ESET LiveGrid cloud-based reputation system launched
First UEFI Scanner in the industry; 30th anniversary
Advanced Machine Learning offline endpoint detection layer released
ESET AI Advisor for XDR incident response launched; revenue reached €691M

awards: Europe's biggest privately held cybersecurity company

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: ESET's core value rests on 30+ years of proprietary threat intelligence data, 847-member R&D team, and deep behavioral analysis expertise that cannot easily be replicated by foundation models. However, commoditization of endpoint detection is real as cloud-native security platforms (Microsoft Defender, CrowdStrike) increasingly bundle AI security at scale, threatening ESET's consumer and SMB moat.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Cybersecurity is a structurally growing market with sustained enterprise demand. ESET has 500k+ business customers, 1B+ consumer users, and €691M revenue with 9% YoY growth, confirming real PMF in an established market.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: ESET is a self-funded, profitable private company with €100M adjusted EBITDA (2024) and three consecutive decades of growth. No external funding needed.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: ESET's profitability, €691M revenue base, proprietary threat intelligence moat, and 30-year track record make it resilient. Medium AGI displacement risk is offset by deep data assets and regulated enterprise relationships.

metadata.notes: ESET is headquartered in Bratislava, Slovakia, but has a significant Czech presence: ESET software spol. s r.o. at Classic 7 Business Park, Jankovcova 1037/49, Praha 7 (sales/marketing), and ESET Research Czech Republic s.r.o. in Brno (R&D). Revenue figure converted from €691M at ~1.10 USD/EUR. Czech CZ entity has 50-99 employees (2023, EMIS data). Global employees estimated 1001-5000 based on LeadIQ and Wikipedia 2020 data of 1,831.
